title: SurfaceContext
description: Describes the connecting surface so the gateway can adapt its output. Sent inside the ATTACH_SESSION WebSocket frame. All fields are OPTIONAL; servers MUST tolerate missing or unknown values.

device_type.description: Well-known device type or any future string value. Unknown values MUST be treated as 'unknown'.

max_output_tokens.description: Output length hint. null means no limit. Servers SHOULD respect this as a target, not a hard cap.

ui_capabilities.description: Capabilities the surface can render. Well-known values: 'markdown', 'tables', 'images', 'audio', 'streaming'. Unknown values MUST be ignored.

locale.description: BCP-47 locale code for language and formatting preferences.

surface_id.description: Optional stable device fingerprint. Allows the server to recognize returning surfaces and correlate session history.

mcp_tools.description: MCP tool names exposed by this surface. The gateway MAY inject corresponding tool schemas into the LLM context and WILL route TOOL_CALL frames for these tools to the surface.
